steps.title: Move the t term
steps.content:
Start with the equation and isolate the term that contains $n$.
$$\frac{20}{n}-\frac{4}{t}=-\frac{4}{w}$$
$$\frac{20}{n}=\frac{4}{t}-\frac{4}{w}$$

steps.title: Combine the fractions
steps.content:
Use the common denominator $tw$ on the right side.
$$\frac{4}{t}-\frac{4}{w}=\frac{4w-4t}{tw}$$
$$=\frac{4(w-t)}{tw}$$

steps.title: Isolate the target variable
steps.content: Now solve the proportion. $$\frac{20}{n}=\frac{4(w-t)}{tw}$$ $$20tw=4n(w-t)$$ $$5tw=n(w-t)$$ $$n=\frac{5tw}{w-t}$$ The answer is D.
